raw:
The test references planner.md crew prompt and index.ts wiring. Let me check those.
Let me verify constraints via git diff to confirm no forbidden files changed.
The work isn't committed yet. Let me check uncommitted changes for the planner work and confirm no forbidden files modified.
gates.ts is not modified — good. Let me check the ledger.ts modification since it could touch ActivityPhase, and confirm the planner uses valid ActivityPhase values.
All constraints look satisfied. Let me confirm the full test suite passes and gate_flow_test still works.
gate_flow_test.sh **failed**. The task explicitly requires keeping `gate_flow_test` working. Let me investigate.
gate_flow_test failed. Let me inspect the kept logs to determine if this is a regression or environmental/model flakiness.
This is the kept temp dir from the failed run. The planner timed out (fallback worked correctly), but the driver model called foreman 29 times in the single `gate1_start` sync call, driving it all the way to `done`. That looks like driver-model flakiness rather than a planner logic bug. Let me re-run to check determinism.
Reproducible failure. Let me understand the 29 foreman calls in this run.
The deterministic verify suite passes. Let me confirm the failure is driver-model flakiness, not a planner defect, by checking what foreman actually returned.
Two consecutive failures with the same mode. The driver model calls `foreman` multiple times in one sync turn, driving to `done`. Let me determine whether this is a pre-existing condition or caused by the planner change by testing the committed (pre-planner) code.
The planner adds a 30s blocking planner-agent call before Gate 1 returns. Let me check whether gate_flow_test passed on the committed (pre-planner) version to determine if this is a regression.
Important finding: the **committed (pre-planner) version PASSES** gate_flow_test, but the planner version fails reproducibly. This is a regression. Let me restore the planner files and investigate the cause.
